text: This paper investigates the capacity of large language models (LLMs) in understanding geometries and their spatial relationships. We employ LLMs like GPT-2 and BERT to train geometry classes, geometric attributes and spatial relationships in the well-known text (WKT) format of geometries. And we train classifiers and regressors based on the embeddings of LLMs-generated WKT for various geometric attributes. The experimental results show that the generated embeddings by LLMs have a good capability for retaining geometry types and understanding some spatial relationships, achieving an average accuracy of up to 73%; However, they have poor performance for predicting numerical attributes and retrieving spatial related objects. Further work is urgently needed for foundation models to learn the geometric attributes and spatial relationships in geospatial data in a more subtle way, and to incorporate domain knowledge for better realization of GeoAI applications.
